seoTitle: Runic Analog Products | Op-Amps, ADCs, Power Management | LiTong Distributor
seoDescription: Explore Runic operational amplifiers, data converters, power management ICs, and interface products. Authorized distributor with technical support and selection guide.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by Runic?
faqs.answer: Runic offers four main analog product categories: (1) Operational Amplifiers - precision, low-power, and high-speed op-amps for signal conditioning with offset voltages as low as 5μV; (2) Data Converters - high-resolution ADCs up to 24-bit and precision DACs for measurement applications; (3) Power Management - LDOs with ultra-low dropout, DC-DC converters, and precision voltage references; (4) Interface Circuits - RS-485/RS-422 transceivers, CAN bus interfaces, and level translators. These products serve industrial, medical, automotive, and consumer markets.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review each product category to find components matching your analog signal chain requirements.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Runic operational amplifier?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Runic op-amp requires considering: (1) Precision - offset voltage (5μV to 1mV range), drift, and noise for your accuracy requirements; (2) Bandwidth - GBW from 10kHz to 50MHz depending on signal frequency; (3) Power consumption - ultra-low power (<1μA) for battery applications or high-speed (mA range) for performance; (4) Supply voltage - single-supply (1.8V to 5.5V) or dual-supply (±18V) operation; (5) Package - SOT23, MSOP, or SOIC depending on space constraints. Runic offers pin-compatible alternatives to popular TI and ADI op-amps.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our op-amp selection guide or contact FAE with your precision, bandwidth, and power requirements.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Runic's precision and high-speed op-amps?
faqs.answer: Runic precision op-amps prioritize DC accuracy and low noise: (1) Offset voltage as low as 5μV with minimal drift; (2) Low 1/f noise for accurate low-frequency measurements; (3) Moderate bandwidth (10kHz-1MHz) suitable for sensor conditioning; (4) Higher supply current (0.5-2mA) for better linearity. High-speed op-amps prioritize AC performance: (1) High GBW (10-50MHz) for fast signal processing; (2) High slew rate for large signal bandwidth; (3) Lower precision (100μV-1mV offset) acceptable for AC applications; (4) Higher supply current (2-10mA) for speed. Choose precision for sensor/DC applications, high-speed for signal processing/filtering.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose precision op-amps for sensor conditioning and measurement, high-speed for active filters and signal processing.

faqs.question: Which Runic data converter should I choose for my application?
faqs.answer: Runic ADC selection depends on your requirements: (1) Resolution - 12-bit for general purpose, 16-bit for precision, 24-bit for high-resolution measurement; (2) Speed - SAR ADCs for fast sampling (100ksps-1Msps), Sigma-Delta for high resolution at lower speeds; (3) Interface - SPI or I2C depending on your MCU; (4) Power - low-power options for battery applications. For DACs: (1) Resolution - 8/10/12-bit options; (2) Output - voltage or current output; (3) Interface - parallel or serial. Runic offers pin-compatible alternatives to ADI and TI converters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Define your resolution, speed, and interface requirements, then consult our ADC/DAC selection guide.

faqs.question: What power management products does Runic offer?
faqs.answer: Runic power management portfolio includes: (1) LDOs - low-dropout regulators with 1.8V-5.5V input, ultra-low quiescent current (<1μA) for battery applications, and high PSRR for noise-sensitive circuits; (2) Voltage References - precision references with 0.1% accuracy and low drift (10ppm/°C) for ADCs and DACs; (3) Supervisors - voltage monitors and reset circuits for system reliability; (4) Load Switches - power distribution switches with controlled slew rate. All products offer thermal shutdown and current limiting protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select LDOs based on dropout voltage, current requirements, and PSRR needs. Contact FAE for power tree design assistance.

categories.name: Operational Amplifiers
categories.description: Runic operational amplifiers deliver precision, low-power, and high-speed performance for signal conditioning applications. The portfolio includes zero-drift, precision, low-power, and high-speed families.
categories.longDescription: Runic operational amplifiers are designed for precision signal conditioning in industrial, medical, and automotive applications. As an authorized Runic distributor, LiTong provides comprehensive op-amp selection guidance, circuit design support, and application engineering services. The product range includes zero-drift op-amps with microvolt-level offset, ultra-low-power op-amps for battery applications, and high-speed op-amps for active filtering. These products offer competitive performance with industry leaders at significantly lower cost.

series.name: RS8xx Series
series.description: Zero-drift precision op-amps with 5μV max offset

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Runic Operational Amplifiers
selectionGuide.description: Consider offset voltage, bandwidth, power consumption, and package when selecting Runic op-amps.

faqs.question: What offset voltage can Runic op-amps achieve?
faqs.answer: Runic offers op-amps with industry-leading offset voltage specifications: (1) Zero-drift series (RS8xx) - 5μV max offset voltage with auto-zero calibration; (2) Precision series (RS7xx) - 50μV max offset for general precision applications; (3) General purpose (RS1xx/2xx) - 200μV-1mV offset for cost-sensitive designs. The zero-drift op-amps use chopper-stabilized or auto-zero topology to maintain low offset over temperature and time. Offset drift is as low as 0.01μV/°C for the best devices, making them ideal for high-gain instrumentation applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-gain applications (>100x), choose zero-drift. For general purpose, precision series offers good value.

faqs.question: What is the GBW range of Runic op-amps?
faqs.answer: Runic op-amps cover a wide GBW range to suit different applications: (1) Low-bandwidth (<100kHz) - for sensor conditioning and slow signals; (2) Medium-bandwidth (100kHz-1MHz) - for general-purpose signal processing; (3) High-bandwidth (1-10MHz) - for audio and medium-speed filtering; (4) Very high bandwidth (>10MHz) - for high-speed signal processing and video. The RS6xx high-speed series offers up to 50MHz GBW with 30V/μs slew rate, suitable for active filters and fast signal conditioning. Choose bandwidth 5-10x higher than your signal frequency for adequate gain margin.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select GBW based on your signal frequency and required closed-loop gain. Use GBW = Gain × Bandwidth × 10 for margin.

faqs.question: How low is the power consumption of Runic low-power op-amps?
faqs.answer: Runic ultra-low-power op-amps consume minimal power for battery applications: (1) RS3xx series - <1μA supply current per amplifier at 5V; (2) RS2xx series - 10-50μA for low-power general purpose; (3) RS1xx series - 100-500μA for standard applications. The ultra-low-power devices achieve this through advanced CMOS design and power-down modes. Even at <1μA, these op-amps maintain reasonable bandwidth (10-100kHz) and offset voltage (50-200μV), making them suitable for sensor conditioning in IoT and wearable devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: For battery applications with continuous operation, choose RS3xx. For intermittent operation, RS2xx offers good balance.

faqs.question: What packages are available for Runic op-amps?
faqs.answer: Runic op-amps are available in industry-standard packages: (1) SOT23-5 - single op-amp in smallest footprint (2.9×2.8mm); (2) SC70-5 - even smaller (2.0×2.1mm) for space-constrained designs; (3) MSOP-8 - dual op-amp in compact 3×3mm; (4) SOIC-8 - industry standard for easy prototyping; (5) TSSOP-14 - quad op-amp for high density. All packages are RoHS compliant and support standard SMT assembly. The small packages (SOT23, SC70) enable high channel density in portable devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SOT23 or SC70 for space-constrained designs, SOIC for prototyping, MSOP for balanced size and solderability.

faqs.question: Are Runic op-amps pin-compatible with other brands?
faqs.answer: Yes, many Runic op-amps are designed to be pin-compatible and functionally equivalent to popular international brands: (1) RS8551/8552/8554 - pin-compatible with OPAx189, ADAx4522; (2) RS6331/6332 - pin-compatible with OPAx354, ADAx803; (3) RS8751/8752 - pin-compatible with OPAx365, ADAx803; (4) RS321/358/324 - pin-compatible with LM321, LM358, LM324. This allows drop-in replacement without PCB redesign. Electrical specifications are comparable or better in most cases. Runic provides detailed cross-reference guides for migration from TI, ADI, Maxim, and other brands.
faqs.decisionGuide: Check Runic's cross-reference guide for direct replacements or contact FAE for migration support.

products.name: Zero-Drift Precision Op-Amp
products.shortDescription: Zero-drift precision op-amp with 5μV max offset voltage, 4.5MHz GBW, and 20μA supply current for high-accuracy applic...
descriptionParagraphs: The RS8551 is a zero-drift precision operational amplifier featuring auto-zero calibration to achieve 5μV maximum offset voltage.
With 4.5MHz gain-bandwidth product and 2.5V/μs slew rate, it provides excellent AC performance while maintaining DC precision.
The device operates from 2.7V to 5.5V single supply and consumes only 20μA per amplifier, making it ideal for portable instrumentation.

faeReview.author: Dr. Wang Wei
faeReview.title: Senior Analog FAE
faeReview.content: The RS8551 has become my go-to recommendation for precision applications requiring zero-drift performance. I've used it in numerous current sensing and sensor conditioning designs, and it consistently delivers the advertised 5μV offset. The auto-zero architecture effectively eliminates 1/f noise, which is crucial for low-frequency measurements. What impresses me most is the combination of precision and reasonable power consumption - 20μA is excellent for a zero-drift op-amp. The pin-compatibility with OPA189 makes it an easy drop-in replacement with significant cost savings. For high-side current sensing with gains of 50-100, this op-amp's low offset minimizes the error contribution.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent zero-drift performance with 5μV offset at competitive cost

alternativeParts.reason: Runic offers equivalent performance at lower cost with better local support
Runic offers higher bandwidth and lower power consumption
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications requiring both precision and moderate speed

faqs.question: How does the auto-zero calibration work in RS8551?
faqs.answer: The RS8551 uses an auto-zero calibration technique that continuously corrects offset voltage: (1) Internal switches alternate between normal operation and calibration phases at ~10kHz; (2) During calibration, the offset is measured and stored on internal capacitors; (3) During normal operation, this correction is applied to cancel the offset; (4) The result is effective elimination of DC offset and 1/f noise. The calibration is transparent to the user and requires no external components. The switching creates small charge injection glitches, but these are filtered internally and typically don't affect the application.
faqs.decisionGuide: No user intervention required. The auto-zero operates automatically. Just ensure power supply bypassing is adequate.

faqs.question: What is the recommended PCB layout for RS8551?
faqs.answer: For optimal RS8551 performance: (1) Place 0.1μF ceramic bypass capacitor within 2mm of each supply pin; (2) Use solid ground plane under the op-amp for noise shielding; (3) Keep input traces short and away from digital/high-current traces; (4) Use guard rings around input pins for high-impedance applications (>1MΩ); (5) Minimize thermocouple effects by keeping copper areas symmetrical. For precision applications, pay special attention to solder joints and connector thermal EMF. The auto-zero architecture helps reject some of these errors, but good layout practices still matter.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow standard precision analog layout practices. Use guard rings for high-impedance sensor interfaces.

faqs.question: Can RS8551 be used for high-side current sensing?
faqs.answer: Yes, the RS8551 is excellent for high-side current sensing: (1) Rail-to-rail input allows operation at supply voltage; (2) Low 5μV offset minimizes error at low sense voltages; (3) 4.5MHz GBW provides fast response for current limiting; (4) 20μA current consumption is suitable for battery applications. For a typical 100mΩ sense resistor at 1A, the 100mV signal with 5μV offset error gives 0.005% accuracy. The auto-zero feature maintains this accuracy over temperature. Use a differential amplifier configuration with matched resistors for best CMRR.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use differential configuration with 0.1% resistors. Place sense resistor close to op-amp inputs.

faqs.question: What is the noise performance of RS8551?
faqs.answer: The RS8551 has excellent noise performance: (1) Voltage noise density - 25nV/√Hz at 1kHz; (2) Low 1/f noise corner - ~100Hz due to auto-zero; (3) Current noise - 5fA/√Hz (negligible for most applications); (4) 0.1-10Hz peak-to-peak noise - 0.5μVpp. The auto-zero architecture effectively eliminates 1/f (flicker) noise, which is a major advantage for DC and low-frequency applications. For a 1kHz bandwidth, total noise is approximately 0.8μVrms. This is excellent for a zero-drift op-amp and comparable to or better than competing devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: The low 1/f noise makes RS8551 ideal for low-frequency sensor applications. Calculate total noise for your bandwidth.

faqs.question: How does RS8551 compare to chopper-stabilized op-amps?
faqs.answer: RS8551 uses auto-zero rather than traditional chopper stabilization: (1) Auto-zero has lower switching noise/intermodulation than chopper; (2) Both achieve similar offset voltage (5μV range); (3) Auto-zero typically has wider bandwidth for the same power; (4) Chopper may have slightly better offset drift in some cases; (5) Auto-zero is generally preferred for applications sensitive to switching artifacts. The RS8551's auto-zero implementation is optimized to minimize charge injection and switching noise, making it suitable for precision measurement applications where chopper noise might be problematic.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose RS8551 for applications sensitive to switching noise. Both architectures provide excellent DC precision.

products.name: High-Speed Op-Amp
products.shortDescription: High-speed op-amp with 50MHz GBW, 30V/μs slew rate, and 2.5mA supply current for fast signal processing applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS6331 is a high-speed operational amplifier featuring 50MHz gain-bandwidth product and 30V/μs slew rate.
With rail-to-rail input and output, it maximizes dynamic range in single-supply applications.
The device is stable at unity gain and drives capacitive loads up to 100pF, making it ideal for active filters and video buffering.

faeReview.author: Li Ming
faeReview.title: Signal Chain Applications Engineer
faeReview.content: The RS6331 is my recommendation for customers needing high-speed performance without breaking the budget. At 50MHz GBW, it handles most signal processing tasks in industrial and medical equipment. I've successfully used it in 16-bit ADC driver applications where settling time is critical - the 100ns to 0.1% is excellent for this class of op-amp. The 30V/μs slew rate ensures clean reproduction of fast edges. One thing to note - the 2.5mA supply current is higher than precision op-amps, so it's not suitable for ultra-low-power applications. But for performance-critical designs, this is a great value proposition.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent speed-performance ratio for cost-sensitive high-speed designs

alternativeParts.reason: Runic offers good performance at significantly lower cost and power

faqs.question: What is the settling time of RS6331?
faqs.answer: The RS6331 settling time is 100ns to 0.1% for a 2V step. This is excellent for a 50MHz op-amp and makes it suitable for driving high-resolution ADCs. Settling time depends on: (1) Step size - larger steps take longer; (2) Closed-loop gain - higher gain reduces effective bandwidth and increases settling; (3) Load capacitance - heavy capacitive loads slow settling; (4) Input source impedance - high impedance interacts with input capacitance. For ADC driving applications, ensure the op-amp settles to better than 0.5 LSB before ADC sampling starts.
faqs.decisionGuide: Allow 2-3x settling time in your timing budget. Use SPICE simulation for precise timing analysis.

faqs.question: Can RS6331 drive large capacitive loads?
faqs.answer: The RS6331 can drive capacitive loads up to 100pF at unity gain without external compensation. For larger loads: (1) Use isolation resistor (10-50Ω) in series with output; (2) Implement snubber network for very large loads; (3) Consider reducing loop gain for capacitive load driving. The high 60mA output drive capability helps with capacitive load charging. For video applications with long coaxial cables, the output drive capability ensures good signal integrity. Always check stability with your specific load using SPICE simulation or bench testing.
faqs.decisionGuide: For loads >100pF, add series isolation resistor and verify stability with network analyzer.

categories.name: Data Converters
categories.description: Runic data converters provide high-resolution analog-to-digital and digital-to-analog conversion for precision measurement and control applications. The portfolio includes SAR ADCs, delta-sigma ADCs, and precision DACs.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Runic Data Converters
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting ADCs and DACs

faqs.question: What is the difference between SAR and delta-sigma ADCs?
faqs.answer: SAR ADCs provide fast conversion with medium resolution (12-16 bits) and are ideal for multiplexed channels. Delta-sigma ADCs offer higher resolution (24+ bits) with slower speeds, perfect for precision measurement.

products.name: 16-Bit 1MSPS SAR ADC
products.shortDescription: High-performance 16-bit SAR ADC with 1MSPS sampling rate, SPI interface, and low power consumption.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS1240 is a 16-bit successive approximation register (SAR) ADC featuring 1MSPS sampling rate and SPI interface. It provides excellent DC and AC performance for data acquisition applications.
With ±1 LSB INL/DNL, 90dB SNR, and low 10mW power consumption, the RS1240 delivers high-resolution conversion with minimal power. The pseudo-differential input accepts 0-VREF signals.
The ADC operates from a single 2.7V-5.25V supply and includes an internal reference. Available in compact TSSOP-16 and QFN-16 packages.

faeReview.author: Dr. Wang Wei
faeReview.title: Senior Analog FAE
faeReview.content: The RS1240 is an excellent general-purpose 16-bit ADC. The 1MSPS rate and SPI interface make it easy to integrate with most microcontrollers. I've used it in numerous industrial control applications with great results.

products.name: 12-Bit 2MSPS Low-Power ADC
products.shortDescription: Low-power 12-bit SAR ADC with 2MSPS sampling rate for battery-powered applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS1220 is a 12-bit SAR ADC optimized for low-power applications. It features 2MSPS sampling rate with only 5mW power consumption.
With ±0.5 LSB INL/DNL and 70dB SNR, the RS1220 provides good performance for cost-sensitive applications. The ADC includes power-down modes for battery operation.
Operates from 2.7V-3.6V supply with SPI interface. Available in small SOT23-8 package.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE
faeReview.content: The RS1220 is perfect for battery-powered applications. The low power consumption and small package make it ideal for portable devices.

products.name: 24-Bit Delta-Sigma ADC
products.shortDescription: High-resolution 24-bit delta-sigma ADC with PGA for precision measurement applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS2420 is a 24-bit delta-sigma ADC featuring integrated PGA and precision reference. It delivers exceptional resolution for weigh scales and sensor applications.
With programmable gain (1-128), 20nV input-referred noise, and 22 effective bits, the RS2420 enables precise measurement of small signals. The ADC includes 50/60Hz notch filters.
Operates from 2.7V-5.25V with SPI interface. Available in TSSOP-20 package.

faeReview.author: Dr. Wang Wei
faeReview.title: Senior Analog FAE
faeReview.content: The RS2420 delivers excellent precision for weigh scale and sensor applications. The integrated PGA and filters simplify design while maintaining high accuracy.

products.name: 16-Bit 4-Channel ADC
products.shortDescription: 16-bit 4-channel SAR ADC with simultaneous sampling for multi-channel data acquisition.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS1640 is a 4-channel 16-bit SAR ADC with simultaneous sampling capability. It enables precise multi-channel data acquisition for industrial control.
With 500kSPS per channel, ±1 LSB linearity, and simultaneous sampling, the RS1640 is ideal for motor control and power monitoring. The ADC includes sample-and-hold circuits.
Operates from 4.75V-5.25V with SPI interface. Available in TSSOP-24 package.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE
faeReview.content: The RS1640 is excellent for multi-channel applications. The simultaneous sampling ensures phase accuracy between channels for motor control.

categories.name: Voltage References
categories.description: Runic voltage references provide stable, accurate reference voltages for data converters and precision measurement applications. The portfolio includes bandgap and buried Zener references.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Runic Voltage References
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting precision voltage references

faqs.question: What is the difference between bandgap and Zener references?
faqs.answer: Bandgap references use the temperature-stable bandgap voltage of silicon (~1.25V) and can operate at low voltages. Zener references use avalanche breakdown and offer better long-term stability but require higher supply voltages.

products.name: 2.5V Precision Voltage Reference
products.shortDescription: High-precision 2.5V bandgap reference with 0.1% accuracy and 10ppm/C tempco.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS6220 is a precision 2.5V bandgap voltage reference featuring 0.1% initial accuracy and 10ppm/C temperature coefficient. It provides stable reference for data converters.
With low 10μVp-p noise and excellent line/load regulation, the RS6220 ensures accurate conversions. The device operates with supply voltages from 2.7V to 18V.
Available in SOT23-3 and TO-92 packages. No external components required.

faeReview.author: Dr. Wang Wei
faeReview.title: Senior Analog FAE
faeReview.content: The RS6220 is a reliable 2.5V reference for general-purpose applications. The 0.1% accuracy and 10ppm tempco provide good performance at a competitive price.

products.name: 4.096V High-Precision Reference
products.shortDescription: Ultra-precision 4.096V reference with 0.05% accuracy and 5ppm/C tempco for high-resolution ADCs.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS6240 is an ultra-precision 4.096V bandgap reference featuring 0.05% initial accuracy and 5ppm/C temperature coefficient. It provides stable reference for high-resolution data converters.
With ultra-low 5μVp-p noise and exceptional line/load regulation, the RS6240 ensures maximum accuracy from 16-bit and 24-bit converters. The 4.096V output provides convenient LSB sizing.
Operates from 5V to 18V supply. Available in SOIC-8 package with enhanced thermal performance.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE
faeReview.content: The RS6240 is excellent for high-resolution applications. The 5ppm tempco and low noise ensure the reference doesn't limit converter performance.

products.name: 1.25V Low-Power Reference
products.shortDescription: Low-power 1.25V bandgap reference with 0.2% accuracy for battery-powered applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS6210 is a low-power 1.25V bandgap reference optimized for battery-powered applications. It features 0.2% accuracy with only 50μA supply current.
With 20ppm/C tempco and low dropout voltage, the RS6210 operates from supplies as low as 1.8V. The shutdown mode reduces current to 1μA.
Available in tiny SC70-5 package for space-constrained designs.

faeReview.author: Dr. Wang Wei
faeReview.title: Senior Analog FAE
faeReview.content: The RS6210 is perfect for battery applications. The low power consumption and tiny package make it ideal for portable designs.

products.name: 5V Low-Noise Reference
products.shortDescription: Low-noise 5V precision reference with 3ppm/C tempco for high-performance applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS6350 is a low-noise 5V precision reference featuring 3ppm/C temperature coefficient and excellent long-term stability. It provides stable reference for precision analog systems.
With ultra-low 3μVp-p noise (0.1-10Hz) and 0.02% accuracy, the RS6350 ensures minimal reference noise in sensitive measurements. The device includes trim pins for fine adjustment.
Operates from 7V to 18V supply. Available in SOIC-8 package.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE
faeReview.content: The RS6350 is Runic's highest performance reference. The 3ppm tempco and ultra-low noise make it suitable for the most demanding applications.
faeReview.highlight: Ultra-low noise reference for precision systems

categories.name: Power Management
categories.description: Runic power management ICs provide efficient voltage regulation for battery-powered and industrial applications. The portfolio includes LDOs, DC-DC converters, and battery chargers.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Runic Power Management ICs
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting LDOs and DC-DC converters

faqs.question: When should I use an LDO vs a switching regulator?
faqs.answer: Use LDOs when the input-output voltage difference is small (<1V) and low noise is critical. Use switching regulators for higher efficiency when the voltage difference is large or for battery applications.

products.name: 1.8V 300mA Ultra-Low-Noise LDO
products.shortDescription: Ultra-low-noise LDO with 300mA output, 200mV dropout, and 80dB PSRR for sensitive analog circuits.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS7118 is an ultra-low-noise LDO providing 1.8V output at up to 300mA. It features 200mV dropout voltage at full load and exceptional 80dB PSRR at 1kHz.
With only 10μVRMS noise (10Hz-100kHz), the RS7118 is ideal for powering sensitive analog circuits and data converters. The enable pin allows power sequencing.
Operates from 2.5V to 5.5V input. Available in SOT23-5 and SC70-5 packages.

faeReview.author: Dr. Wang Wei
faeReview.title: Senior Analog FAE
faeReview.content: The RS7118 is excellent for powering sensitive analog circuits. The ultra-low noise and high PSRR ensure clean power for precision measurements.

products.name: 3.3V 500mA Low-Iq LDO
products.shortDescription: Low-quiescent-current LDO with 500mA output and 1μA shutdown current for battery applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS7133 is a low-Iq LDO providing 3.3V output at up to 500mA. It features only 25μA quiescent current and 1μA shutdown current for battery-powered applications.
With 250mV dropout at 500mA and 60dB PSRR, the RS7133 provides efficient regulation for digital and analog circuits. The device includes thermal and current limiting.
Operates from 3.8V to 6V input. Available in SOT23-5 and SOT89 packages.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE
faeReview.content: The RS7133 is perfect for battery applications. The low Iq and shutdown current maximize battery life in portable devices.

products.name: 2MHz 1A Buck Converter
products.shortDescription: High-efficiency 2MHz buck converter with 1A output for battery and industrial applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS8120 is a synchronous buck converter featuring 2MHz switching frequency and up to 1A output current. It delivers high efficiency for battery-powered applications.
With up to 95% efficiency and 1μA shutdown current, the RS8120 maximizes battery life. The high switching frequency enables small inductor size.
Operates from 2.7V to 5.5V input with adjustable 0.6V to Vin output. Available in SOT23-6 package.

faeReview.author: Dr. Wang Wei
faeReview.title: Senior Analog FAE
faeReview.content: The RS8120 is an efficient buck converter for battery applications. The 2MHz frequency allows small inductors while maintaining good efficiency.
faeReview.highlight: High-efficiency buck converter for battery apps

products.name: 600kHz 3A Synchronous Buck
products.shortDescription: High-current 600kHz buck converter with 3A output for industrial and consumer applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS8150 is a high-current synchronous buck converter featuring 600kHz switching and up to 3A output current. It provides efficient power conversion for demanding loads.
With up to 96% efficiency and integrated MOSFETs, the RS8150 minimizes external components. The device includes soft-start, thermal protection, and current limiting.
Operates from 4.5V to 18V input with adjustable 0.8V to 0.9xVin output. Available in SOIC-8 with exposed pad.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE
faeReview.content: The RS8150 is a robust buck converter for industrial applications. The 3A capability and wide input range make it versatile for various designs.
